evals.prompt: I want to build a book reading app. Users should be able to browse a library of books, read them, bookmark pages, and track their reading progress. It needs login/signup. Make me a clickable prototype so I can see all the screens and navigate between them.
evals.expected_output: A single self-contained HTML file with 6+ screens (splash, login, library/home, book detail, reader, profile/settings), working navigation between all screens, smooth transitions, fake book data with real titles/authors, fake auth that validates non-empty fields, and bottom tab navigation. Should look like a polished app when opened in a browser.
expectations: Output is a single .html file saved to ~/.agent/prototypes/
File contains at least 6 distinct screens (section elements with data-screen attribute)
Login screen has email and password inputs with a sign-in button
Navigating from login to home screen works with a transition animation
Home screen shows a list of books with real titles and authors (not Lorem ipsum)
Clicking a book navigates to a detail screen with the book's information
Book detail screen has a 'Read' or 'Start Reading' button that navigates to a reader view
Bottom tab bar is present with at least 3 tabs (Home, Discover/Search, Profile)
Switching tabs navigates to different screens
Both light and dark mode are supported via CSS variables
The file opens without console errors in a browser
Screen transitions use CSS animations (not instant swaps)

evals.prompt: prototype a food delivery app like uber eats. i want to see the full flow - browsing restaurants, picking items, cart, checkout. needs to look good on mobile. auth flow too
evals.expected_output: A mobile-first HTML prototype with screens for: splash, login, home (restaurant list with categories), restaurant detail (menu items), item detail with add-to-cart, cart screen, checkout flow, order confirmation, order tracking, and profile. Should have warm food-themed colors (orange/amber palette), realistic restaurant and food item data, working cart state management, and smooth screen transitions.
expectations: Output is a single .html file
Contains at least 8 screens covering the full food ordering flow
Home screen shows restaurant cards with names, ratings, delivery time, and cuisine type
Tapping a restaurant shows its menu with item names and prices
Cart functionality exists - items can be 'added' and the cart screen shows them
Checkout screen includes delivery address and payment section
Color palette is warm/food-themed (not cold blue or neon)
Layout is mobile-first (works well at 390px width)
Fake food data uses realistic names and prices (not placeholder text)
Navigation transitions are smooth and directional
Bottom navigation tabs are present
Auth flow (login) is implemented with fake validation

evals.prompt: I'm pitching a fintech app to investors next week. It's a personal finance dashboard that shows account balances, recent transactions, spending categories (pie chart or breakdown), and lets users set budgets. Need a professional-looking prototype with all screens connected. Desktop layout preferred.
evals.expected_output: A desktop-first HTML prototype with screens for: login (with PIN/passcode feel), dashboard (account balances, recent transactions, spending summary), transaction list (filterable), transaction detail, budget settings, transfer/pay screen, and account settings. Professional color palette (navy/gold or slate). Desktop layout with sidebar navigation. Realistic financial data (account numbers, transaction merchants, dollar amounts). Polished enough for an investor presentation.
expectations: Output is a single .html file
Contains at least 6 screens covering the finance app flow
Dashboard shows account balance(s) with realistic dollar amounts
Transaction list shows recent transactions with merchant names, dates, and amounts
Spending breakdown is visualized (chart, bars, or categorized summary)
Layout uses a sidebar navigation pattern (desktop-first, not bottom tabs)
Color palette is professional - navy, slate, or similar (not playful/bright)
Financial data looks realistic (real merchant names like 'Starbucks', 'Amazon')
Navigation between all screens works with transitions
Budget or spending limit feature is present
The prototype looks polished enough for a business presentation
Both light and dark mode support
